description: EQT Corporation is a Pittsburgh-based, vertically integrated U.S. natural gas company focused on Appalachian Basin production, gathering, and transmission, with Marcellus Shale assets at the center of its business.

overview.title: Business Snapshot
paragraphs: EQT is not a diversified supermajor; it is a concentrated Appalachian natural gas platform. Its current reporting describes upstream, gathering, and transmission operations focused in the Appalachian Basin, with Marcellus production as the central operating base.

The business model pairs drilling inventory with midstream capacity so EQT can manage cost, takeaway, and commodity exposure inside one system. The moat is geology plus pipes, which is less glamorous than a platform marketplace but considerably harder to uninstall.

overview.title: Product Footprint
paragraphs: The registry seed product, Marcellus natural gas production, should remain the primary product entry. Adjacent product surfaces include Utica development in eastern Ohio, natural gas liquids recovered from produced gas, and midstream gathering and transmission services.

Customer demand should be tracked by use case rather than only by molecule: power generation, industrial consumption, domestic data center demand, residential and commercial gas use, and LNG-linked export exposure all point to different alternative pathways.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: EQT is a useful Free The World entry because natural gas sits at the boundary between commodity infrastructure and energy dependence. Open and decentralized alternatives are not one-to-one product clones; the credible comparison set is demand reduction and substitution through electrified heating, renewable generation, storage, grid flexibility, open energy data, transparent methane monitoring, and community-scale ownership models.

Publication framing should avoid pretending a gas producer can be replaced by a GitHub repo over a weekend. The sharper comparison is where long-lived fossil infrastructure competes with decentralized, auditable, lower-emission energy systems.

overview.title: Research Notes
paragraphs: The first structured sync should verify reserve mix, state-level acreage, production volumes, pipeline capacity, LNG commitments, and whether midstream should be represented as a separate product family or supporting infrastructure.

Environmental review should separate company claims from third-party evidence. Methane emissions, water sourcing and recycling, hydraulic fracturing disclosures, landowner issues, community impacts, and regulatory proceedings need cross-checking against SEC filings, EPA data, state records, and independent monitoring.

sourceSuggestions.title: EQT 2025 Annual Report - Form 10-K
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for reserves, segments, pipeline infrastructure, risks, sales commitments, and management discussion.

sourceSuggestions.title: EQT Operations - Production
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Company source for Marcellus and Utica footprint, acreage claims, horizontal drilling, and hydraulic fracturing descriptions.

sourceSuggestions.title: EQT Investor Relations Overview
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Summarizes current strategy, integrated business model, market positioning, and investor-facing claims.

sourceSuggestions.title: EQT Operations - Midstream
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful for determining how gathering, transmission, and customer-facing midstream services should be represented.

sourceSuggestions.title: EQT ESG Report
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Company source for emissions, water, safety, methane, and net-zero claims that should be cross-checked.

sourceSuggestions.title: U.S. Energy Information Administration - Drilling Productivity Report
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Independent context for Appalachian Basin natural gas production trends and regional comparison.

sourceSuggestions.title: EPA Greenhouse Gas Reporting Program - FLIGHT
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Facility-level greenhouse gas emissions data for checking operational emissions claims.

sourceSuggestions.title: FracFocus Chemical Disclosure Registry
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Public disclosure source for hydraulic fracturing chemicals and well-level completion data.
